name: Theranos
description: Blood-testing startup claiming to run hundreds of tests from a single drop of blood.
financialHealth: Raised billions based on fraudulent claims. No actual revenue from working products.
bullCase: Revolutionizing healthcare, making blood tests cheap and accessible.
bearCase: The technology doesn't work, and the company is highly secretive.
failureReason: Massive fraud, fake technology, and regulatory shutdown.

name: FTX
description: Cryptocurrency exchange and crypto hedge fund.
financialHealth: Massive commingling of customer funds with trading firm Alameda Research.
bullCase: The most trusted and regulated crypto exchange in the world.
bearCase: Balance sheet is propped up by highly illiquid, self-issued tokens.
recentNews: Binance announces intent to sell FTT holdings
failureReason: Crypto fraud, massive mismanagement, and a bank run.

name: Pets.com
description: Online retailer of pet supplies during the dot-com bubble.
financialHealth: Selling products below cost, massive marketing spend, unsustainable burn rate.
bullCase: First-mover advantage in the massive online pet supply market.
bearCase: Shipping heavy bags of dog food is fundamentally unprofitable.
recentNews: Super Bowl ad features sock puppet
failureReason: Dot-com bubble burst, unsustainable business model.

name: WeWork
description: Co-working space provider attempting to brand itself as a tech company.
financialHealth: Massive long-term lease liabilities with short-term rental income. Huge cash burn.
bullCase: Redefining the future of work and community.
bearCase: It's just a real estate company with a tech valuation and terrible governance.
recentNews: S-1 filing reveals massive losses and self-dealing
failureReason: Failed IPO, governance issues, and unsustainable lease obligations.

name: Silicon Valley Bank
description: Commercial bank specializing in lending to technology startups.
financialHealth: Massive unrealized losses on long-term bonds as interest rates rose.
bullCase: The premier bank for the booming tech and VC ecosystem.
bearCase: Highly concentrated deposit base and poor interest rate risk management.
recentNews: VCs advise startups to pull funds
failureReason: Classic bank run triggered by duration mismatch and social media panic.

name: Credit Suisse
description: Global investment bank and financial services firm based in Switzerland.
financialHealth: Years of scandals, massive outflows of client funds, and declining revenue.
bullCase: A historic Swiss banking institution undergoing a massive restructuring.
bearCase: Too many scandals, loss of client trust, and structural unprofitability.
recentNews: Saudi National Bank rules out more capital
failureReason: Loss of confidence, scandals, and forced acquisition by UBS.

name: Bear Stearns
description: Global investment bank and securities trading and brokerage firm.
financialHealth: Massive exposure to toxic subprime mortgages, highly leveraged.
bullCase: Strong trading franchise and historically profitable.
bearCase: Insolvent due to worthless mortgage assets and a freeze in short-term funding.
recentNews: Two Bear Stearns hedge funds collapse
failureReason: Liquidity crisis, subprime mortgage exposure, fire sale to JPMorgan.

name: Washington Mutual
description: Savings bank holding company and the largest savings and loan association.
financialHealth: Huge portfolio of high-risk subprime and Option ARM mortgages defaulting.
bullCase: Large retail footprint and strong consumer deposit base.
bearCase: Mortgage portfolio is toxic and losses are accelerating rapidly.
failureReason: Largest bank failure in US history due to subprime mortgage losses.

name: RadioShack
description: Retailer of consumer electronics, parts, and accessories.
financialHealth: Declining foot traffic, high debt, and inability to compete with online retailers.
bullCase: Convenient neighborhood locations and a shift to mobile phone sales.
bearCase: Irrelevant in the age of Amazon and direct-to-consumer electronics.
failureReason: Digital disruption, failure to adapt to e-commerce.

name: Sears
description: Once the largest retailer in the United States, operating department stores.
financialHealth: Massive debt burden, chronic underinvestment in stores, declining sales.
bullCase: Valuable real estate portfolio and iconic brands.
bearCase: Stores are dying, Amazon is winning, and management is stripping assets.
failureReason: Retail decline, massive debt burden, and mismanagement.

name: MySpace
description: Early social networking website offering interactive, user-submitted network of friends.
financialHealth: Acquired by News Corp, but lost massive market share and ad revenue to Facebook.
bullCase: The dominant social network with strong ties to the music industry.
bearCase: Cluttered interface, slow innovation, and users are fleeing to Facebook.
failureReason: Lost to Facebook due to poor user experience and slow innovation.

name: Yahoo
description: Web services provider, known for its web portal, search engine, and related services.
financialHealth: Declining core business, value propped up entirely by its stake in Alibaba.
bullCase: Valuable Alibaba stake and strong legacy traffic.
bearCase: Lost the search war to Google, missed buying Facebook and Google.
failureReason: Missed multiple acquisitions, lost search dominance to Google.

name: BlackBerry
description: Enterprise software and former smartphone manufacturer.
financialHealth: Rapidly losing market share, declining handset revenue.
bullCase: Unmatched enterprise security and physical keyboards.
bearCase: The iPhone and Android have completely disrupted their business model.
recentNews: Market share drops to single digits
failureReason: Smartphone disruption, failed to adapt to touchscreens and app ecosystems.

name: Nortel
description: Multinational telecommunications and data networking equipment manufacturer.
financialHealth: Massive accounting scandals, restated earnings, and huge debt.
bullCase: A leader in optical networking equipment during the telecom boom.
bearCase: Accounting is fraudulent and the telecom bubble has burst.
failureReason: Accounting scandal and the burst of the telecom bubble.

name: Toys "R" Us
description: International toy, clothing, and baby product retailer.
financialHealth: Crippled by billions in debt from a leveraged buyout, unable to invest in stores.
bullCase: The definitive category killer for toys with strong brand recognition.
bearCase: Cannot compete with Amazon and Walmart while servicing massive LBO debt.
recentNews: Private equity firms acquire Toys R Us
failureReason: LBO debt burden and inability to compete with e-commerce.

name: Microsoft
description: Multinational technology corporation producing computer software, consumer electronics, and PCs.
financialHealth: Massive cash flow, highly profitable cloud transition.
bullCase: Azure is taking massive market share, and enterprise lock-in is unparalleled.
bearCase: Valuation is high, and PC market is declining.

name: Google
description: Multinational technology company focusing on search engine technology, online advertising, and cloud computing.
financialHealth: Incredible ad revenue generation, strong balance sheet.
bullCase: Monopoly in search and dominant position in mobile OS.
bearCase: Regulatory threats and reliance on ad revenue.

name: Meta
description: Social technology company operating Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp.
financialHealth: High margins from targeted advertising, massive user base.
bullCase: Unmatched targeted advertising platform and billions of daily users.
bearCase: Privacy concerns, regulatory scrutiny, and massive metaverse spending.

name: Tesla
description: Automotive and clean energy company designing and manufacturing electric vehicles.
financialHealth: Survived production hell, now highly profitable with strong margins.
bullCase: Leading the EV revolution with superior software and manufacturing.
bearCase: Valuation is disconnected from reality, increasing competition.

name: Nvidia
description: Multinational technology company designing graphics processing units (GPUs).
financialHealth: Explosive revenue growth and massive margins driven by AI demand.
bullCase: The undisputed leader in hardware powering the AI revolution.
bearCase: Demand may be cyclical, and valuation is priced for perfection.

name: Netflix
description: Subscription video on-demand over-the-top streaming service.
financialHealth: Strong subscriber growth, transitioning to positive free cash flow.
bullCase: The dominant global streaming platform with pricing power.
bearCase: Increasing competition from Disney and others, high content costs.

name: Walmart
description: Multinational retail corporation operating a chain of hypermarkets and discount department stores.
financialHealth: Massive scale, consistent revenue, strong dividend payer.
bullCase: Unmatched scale, pricing power, and successful e-commerce pivot.
bearCase: Low margins and intense competition from Amazon.

name: JPMorgan
description: Multinational financial services firm and the largest bank in the US.
financialHealth: Fortress balance sheet, highly profitable across multiple business lines.
bullCase: The best-managed bank in the world, benefiting from higher rates.
bearCase: Macroeconomic headwinds and increased capital requirements.

name: Costco
description: Multinational corporation operating a chain of membership-only big-box retail stores.
financialHealth: Incredibly loyal membership base providing consistent, high-margin revenue.
bullCase: Inflation-resistant business model with high renewal rates.
bearCase: Valuation is very high for a low-margin retailer.
recentNews: Membership renewal rates hit record highs

name: Visa
description: Multinational financial services corporation facilitating electronic funds transfers.
financialHealth: Incredible operating margins, massive toll-bridge business model.
bullCase: A global duopoly benefiting from the secular shift away from cash.
bearCase: Regulatory threats regarding swipe fees and new payment technologies.
